# Performance, Soak, and Memory Protocol

**Date**: 2026-05-27
**Workflow Context**: Release Remediation Sprint 001 / RR-006
**Generated by**: CCGS `perf-profile` + `soak-test` adapted for Codex
**Owners**: QA Lead, Performance Analyst
**Status**: Protocol ready; automated RC4 pilot complete; full 2-hour human soak not started
**Target Scope**: Windows internal package and 432x768 Godot client evidence

---

## Purpose

Define the performance, soak, and memory evidence required before the 0.1.0 release gate can claim long-duration stability.

This protocol does not execute the soak test. A human tester must run the session, record checkpoint data, complete post-session analysis, and file bugs for any S1/S2 issues.

---

## Release Gate Context

Current clean release blockers include:

- No soak test evidence.
- No performance evidence on target hardware.
- No memory/leak evidence.
- Godot target/runtime mismatch remains unresolved until ADR-0002 is accepted or superseded.

Current internal evidence:

- `npm.cmd run check` passes.
- Windows internal export boot smoke passes.
- Visible 432x768 Windows QA evidence exists.
- Current smoke verdict is `PASS WITH WARNINGS`, not clean `PASS`.
- RC4 automated boot timing and 10-minute memory/performance pilot evidence exists at `production/qa/evidence/performance-memory-pilot-rc4-2026-05-28.md`.

---

## Performance Budgets

| Metric | Budget | Source | Status Before Execution |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| FPS | 60 fps target | `.claude/docs/technical-preferences.md` | Not measured in soak |
| Frame time | 16.6 ms | `.claude/docs/technical-preferences.md` | Not measured in soak |
| Viewport | 432x768 logical canvas | `.claude/docs/technical-preferences.md` | Existing visual QA covered |
| Memory ceiling | Not set | No explicit budget found | Must record trend; cannot assert within budget |
| Load time | Not set | No explicit budget found | Must record launch-to-home timing |
| Asset memory risk | Production assets total about 112.7 MB across 505 files | Static file scan | Needs runtime observation |

---

## Static Profiling Targets

These are candidates for observation during runtime profiling. They are not confirmed bottlenecks until measured.

| Area | Location | Why It Matters | Protocol Action |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Per-frame update | `godot-client/scripts/Main.gd` `_process` | Any per-frame logic can affect 16.6 ms budget. | Watch FPS/frame time during idle, page navigation, and repeated actions. |
| Custom drawing | `Main.gd` nested `_draw` implementations | Draw-heavy UI and world rendering may cause frame spikes or redraw churn. | Record FPS and visual stability after repeated page transitions. |
| Texture loading/cache paths | `Main.gd` texture load helpers and production texture caches | Large production asset set can drive memory growth if resources are repeatedly loaded. | Record memory/object count at each checkpoint after page cycling. |
| Feature-page rebuilds | `feature_page_renderer.gd` and page state binders | Rebuilding UI nodes repeatedly can leak references or increase object count. | Cycle all supported pages at every checkpoint and record object count trend. |
| HTTP/API refresh | `player_api_client.gd` | Backend polling/actions may create pending request or signal issues over time. | Run online and offline/fallback paths separately when possible. |

---

## Test Matrix

### Required For RR-006 Completion

| Run | Platform | Build | Duration | Focus | Required Before Clean Release? |
| --- | --- | --- | ---: | --- | --- |
| SOAK-WIN-001 | Windows desktop | Current internal package or next RC package | 2h | all | Yes, for Windows internal release gate |
| PERF-WIN-001 | Windows desktop | Same build as soak | 30m | FPS/frame/memory sampling | Yes |
| BOOT-WIN-001 | Windows desktop | Same build as soak | 5 runs | launch stability and load timing | Yes |

### Deferred / Not Claimed

| Run | Platform | Reason |
| --- | --- | --- |
| SOAK-MOBILE-001 | Physical mobile device | No physical mobile device evidence exists yet. Desktop evidence must not be used to claim mobile store readiness. |
| THERMAL-MOBILE-001 | Physical mobile device | Battery/thermal behavior is not covered by Windows soak. |

---

## Pre-Session Setup

Before starting SOAK-WIN-001:

- [ ] Confirm exact artifact path.
- [ ] Record artifact SHA256 if artifact policy requires it.
- [ ] Confirm build provenance state: internal/debug, release candidate, or public launch candidate.
- [ ] Confirm Godot version strategy state: Godot 4.4 validation, 4.6.3 internal-test exception, or accepted upgrade.
- [ ] Run `npm.cmd run check`.
- [ ] Close unrelated heavy applications where practical.
- [ ] Open Windows Task Manager, Resource Monitor, or equivalent process monitor.
- [ ] If running from Godot editor/debugger, open Debugger Monitors and record memory/object metrics.
- [ ] Start a timer and keep this protocol open for checkpoint logging.

Baseline command checks:

```powershell
npm.cmd run check
Get-Process | Where-Object { $_.ProcessName -like '*LuckyPack*' -or $_.ProcessName -like '*Godot*' } | Select-Object Id,ProcessName,CPU,WorkingSet,PrivateMemorySize
```

---

## Launch Modes

### Preferred Internal Package Launch

Use the packaged launcher when validating the current internal Windows artifact:

```powershell
build\windows\Play-Lucky-Pack-Online.cmd
```

Record the backend URL shown in `build/windows/README-INTERNAL-TEST.md`.

### Console / Headless Boot Check

Use console wrapper for quick boot confirmation only:

```powershell
build\windows\LuckyPackMMORPG.console.exe --headless --quit-after 3
```

This does not replace visible-window soak.

### Godot Runtime Launch

If testing from local Godot runtime, record exact executable:

```powershell
tools\godot-4.6.3\Godot_v4.6.3-stable_win64_console.exe --path godot-client
```

If Godot 4.4 is later provisioned under ADR-0002, replace the command with the accepted Godot 4.4 executable path and record version output.

---

## 2-Hour Soak Protocol

Duration: 2 hours  
Focus: all (memory, stability, balance/fatigue, content exhaustion)  
Checkpoints: T+0, T+20, T+40, T+60, T+80, T+100, T+120

At each checkpoint:

1. Record process memory.
2. Record FPS/frame-time observation if available.
3. Record object count/orphan node count if running with Godot debugger.
4. Navigate through home, role, packs, market, challenge, guild, and system.
5. Trigger safe player actions that do not imply withdrawable value.
6. Confirm no restricted operator/admin/withdrawal workflow appears.
7. Note any visual overlap, input loss, crash, hang, or backend failure.

---


---

## Pass / Warning / Fail Criteria

### PASS

- No crash, hang, or freeze over 2 hours.
- No repeated input loss or severe interaction latency.
- No restricted operator/admin/withdrawal/tax/payout workflow exposure.
- Memory growth is below 20% from baseline after T+20.
- Object count does not grow monotonically across three or more checkpoints without returning near baseline.
- FPS/frame-time observation remains consistent with 60 fps / 16.6 ms budget, or no visible performance degradation is observed if exact FPS tooling is unavailable.
- All supported pages remain navigable at T+120.

### PASS WITH CONCERNS

- Minor memory growth above baseline that stabilizes before T+120.
- Minor frame dips or visual stutters that do not block navigation.
- Minor content fatigue or copy/readability issue.
- Any missing measurement caused by tooling limitation, if the session otherwise remains stable.

### FAIL

- Any crash, hang, or unrecoverable freeze.
- Memory growth above 20% from baseline after T+20 that continues to grow through T+120.
- Object count grows monotonically across three or more checkpoints and does not stabilize.
- Any S1/S2 bug appears.
- Any restricted operator/admin/withdrawal/tax/payout workflow appears on player pages.
- Any page becomes unreachable or unusable.
- Severe performance degradation blocks normal use.

---

---

## Known Limitations

- This protocol does not validate physical mobile device performance.
- This protocol does not validate battery or thermal behavior.
- Memory ceiling is not defined in technical preferences; the first run establishes a baseline trend rather than a hard MB pass/fail budget.
- Exact FPS/frame-time capture depends on available tooling during execution.
- Current Godot version strategy is proposed, not accepted; the protocol must record whether evidence used Godot 4.4, 4.6.3 internal-test exception, or a later accepted strategy.

---

## RR-006 Acceptance Mapping

RR-006 can move from review to done when:

- [x] Protocol defines duration and pass/fail thresholds.
- [x] Protocol defines hardware/environment details to record.
- [x] Protocol defines memory/leak observation method.
- [x] Protocol separates Windows internal package from mobile device validation.
- [x] Evidence paths are defined.
- [ ] QA Lead sign-off is recorded.
- [ ] Performance Analyst sign-off is recorded.
- [ ] A real soak execution log exists.
- [x] Automated RC4 boot timing and short memory trend pilot exists.

This protocol satisfies the planning part of RR-006, and the RC4 pilot adds short-run automated evidence. It does not satisfy the release gate's missing soak/performance/memory evidence until a real 2-hour human-observed execution log is completed.
